name: Barri Agent
description_short: Barri offers check cashing, money transfers, bill payments, and a prepaid Visa debit card with 30+ years of remittance experience serving Latin American communities.
description_long:
Barri is a financial services company with over 30 years of experience in money transfers and remittances, primarily serving Latino communities in the United States. The company has evolved from a traditional check-cashing operation into a multi-service financial platform accessible through physical store locations and mobile applications.

Barri's core service offerings include check cashing with extended hours and competitive rates, international money transfers to Mexico, Central America, and South America, bill payment services, mobile phone recharges across the US and Latin America, and a branded Visa prepaid debit card. The company has partnered with Oportun to offer personal loans ranging from $300 to $10,000, allowing customers to build credit history while covering unexpected expenses. All services are available through their physical store network, website, and dedicated mobile applications for iOS and Android.

Barri distinguishes itself through its focus on underserved immigrant communities and families sending remittances. The company emphasizes ease of use, convenience, and customer service quality. Their mobile application integrates multiple services into a single platform, allowing users to send money, pay bills, and manage accounts without visiting a physical location. Customer reviews highlight reliability, fast service, and responsiveness to customer needs.

While Barri provides valuable financial services to communities with limited banking access, consumers should note that as a check-cashing and money transfer service rather than a bank, it does not offer FDIC-insured deposit accounts or traditional savings products. The company's loan offerings depend on a third-party partnership with Oportun, and specific APR details for these loans are not disclosed on their website. The prepaid debit card, while convenient, typically carries fees for various transactions that traditional bank accounts may not charge.
pros: Extended operating hours for check cashing with no complex requirements
Multi-country money transfer capability (Mexico, Central America, South America)
Integrated mobile app combining transfers, bill pay, and account management
Partnership with Oportun enabling personal loans up to $10,000 for credit building
High customer satisfaction with 4.9-star app rating across iOS and Android
Physical store network providing in-person service and cash handling
Instant mobile phone recharges across US and Latin America
30+ years of established experience in remittance and money transfer services
cons: Not FDIC-insured bank—no deposit protection or traditional savings accounts
Loan APR and fee structure not transparently disclosed on website
Prepaid debit card likely carries transaction and maintenance fees typical of non-bank cards
Service focus limited primarily to Spanish-speaking and Latin American transfer communities
Partnership-dependent lending means approval and terms controlled by third party (Oportun)
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked Latino immigrants needing check cashing and bill payment
Families sending regular remittances to Mexico, Central America, or South America
Spanish-speaking consumers seeking convenient mobile-first financial management
Individuals building credit history through small personal loans without traditional banking
diagnosis: Barri is best for unbanked and underbanked immigrants and their families who regularly send money internationally and need accessible check cashing and bill payment services. The main caveat is that it operates as a money services business rather than a bank, so customers should not expect FDIC insurance, traditional savings accounts, or transparent fee disclosure—and should carefully review all terms before using the Oportun loan partnership.
services: International money transfers (Mexico, Central America, South America)
Mobile phone recharges (US and Latin America)
Mobile app for remote account management

places_enrichment.adr_format_address: 1741 SW 8th St, Miami, FL 33135, USA
